title: Haley Simple Shelves
summary: A basic three-board wall shelf with short end sides, sized to whatever length you need and hung straight through the back into studs.
description:
This is about as simple as a wall shelf gets: a 1x8 top, a 1x4 back, and two short 1x6 end pieces, in whatever length you want the shelf to be. The measurements below use a 24" shelf as a worked example — swap in your own desired length everywhere "24"" appears, and cut the back board 5-1/2" shorter than the top to leave room for the end pieces.

Hang it by screwing straight through the back 1x4 into wall studs rather than relying on anchors alone — a shelf is only as strong as what it's actually fastened to, and studs are what carry real weight over time.

tools.note: Can be replaced with countersunk screws or nails and glue.
Confirms the shelf hangs level once you've located a stud.

materials.name: Pocket-hole screws, countersunk screws, or finish nails

cutList.note: Cut to your own desired shelf length.

steps.title: Attach the back to the sides
steps.body: Attach the back board to the two side pieces. If you're using a pocket-hole jig, also drill pocket holes facing upward on this assembly now, for attaching the top in the next step.
materials: Pocket-hole screws, countersunk screws, or finish nails

steps.title: Attach the top and hang the shelf
steps.body: Attach the top to the sides and back. When you hang the shelf, screw directly through the back 1x4 into the studs in your wall, checking with a level as you go.
materials: Pocket-hole screws, countersunk screws, or finish nails
